        }
      ],
      "reason": "Original bucket 1 ('start', 'continue', 'finish') has extremely low mass and high ratio; splitting into numeric vs. temporal sequences isolates the failure mode and leverages high-frequency sequencing tokens."

          ],
          "slot_type": "bullet_or_step_opener",
          "why_mass_may_improve": "Groups semantically similar high-mass verbs. 'Check/verify' and 'select/choose' are distinct cognitive actions with strong, separate clusters in instruction tuning data, likely improving bucket mass concentration."
        }
      ],
      "reason": "Original buckets mixed disparate actions (e.g., 'list' with 'check'). Grouping by cognitive operation (audit vs. select) aligns better with Qwen's internal representation of instruction steps."
